modules.name: Prospect
modules.description: Prospect theory (Kahneman & Tversky 1979/1992). Transforms incoming objective outcome CV (gains and losses relative to a reference point) and objective probability CV into subjective value U(x)·w(p), with loss aversion λ, diminishing sensitivity α, and probability weighting γ. Visualizes both the value function and the probability-weighting function. Pedagogical demonstration of why people overweight rare events, prefer certainty, and feel losses ~2× as strongly as equivalent gains.

modules.name: Bandit
modules.description: Multi-armed bandit with up to 8 arms. Right-click selects the action policy: ε-greedy (explore vs exploit), UCB1 (upper confidence bound), or Thompson sampling (Bayesian). Each arm's true reward μ_i is randomized at reset; the agent pulls one arm per tick. Visualizes per-arm running mean estimate, pull count, and cumulative regret. Teaches the exploration–exploitation trade-off, the role of confidence bounds, and Bayesian decision-making, all on one screen.

modules.name: DDM
modules.description: Drift-diffusion (Ratcliff) choice model. An accumulator integrates noisy evidence with drift rate v (true signal strength) toward two absorbing boundaries at ±a. The trial ends when the accumulator hits a boundary; the choice is determined by which one was hit and the time elapsed is the reaction time. Outputs the live evidence trace, choice gate (high if upper threshold reached), reaction-time CV, and running mean accuracy. The standard cognitive-psychology model of two-alternative forced-choice.
